expectedDifferences.reason: The proxy generates stable session-local synthetic IDs for locally staged inventory shipments; Shopify assigns store IDs when live shipment writes are available.
The proxy generates stable session-local synthetic IDs for locally staged shipment line items; Shopify assigns store IDs when live shipment writes are available.
The proxy generates stable session-local synthetic IDs for locally staged inventory shipments; Shopify assigns store IDs when live shipment writes are available.
The proxy generates stable session-local synthetic IDs for locally staged shipment line items; Shopify assigns store IDs when live shipment writes are available.

notes: HAR-306 executable parity now runs the product-backed create-in-transit shipment request, immediate shipment detail read, and downstream InventoryItem quantity read against the local proxy harness. Live 2025-01 schema introspection confirmed the shipment roots and fields, but direct Shopify payload capture for read/write shipment roots is currently blocked because the available conformance grant lacks `read_inventory_shipments` / `write_inventory_shipments`; the fixture preserves that blocker while the runtime integration test covers the full add/remove/update/tracking/receive/delete lifecycle, validation guardrails, no upstream writes, and original raw mutation log order. This scenario is local-runtime-backed runtime fixture evidence only, not Shopify parity evidence; it remains checked in to guard the supported no-upstream local staging contract with Rust integration coverage.
